Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log of 5 cases against a normative process description for handling orders I need to identify which cases deviate from the intended flow and explain the violations based on the provided text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Step 2 Check inventory must occur after Receive order and before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment Branch 1 Sufficient Inventory Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraints Authorize payment only after Check inventory and before packaging Package items only after authorization and before shipping Ship order only after packaging Branch 2 Insufficient Inventory Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Constraints After cancellation it is illogical to continue with fulfillment activities packaging shipping Close order is the final activity in all scenarios General Rules Constraints explicitly stated Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios Deviations like shipping after cancellation authorizing payment before checking inventory or packaging before authorization are violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Matches Branch 1 exactly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Violation Package items occurs before Authorize payment The text says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Also Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging So this violates the order of payment authorization and packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Violations Cancel order occurs after Authorize payment The text says if inventory is insufficient it should go directly to Cancel order after checking inventory Authorizing payment before cancellation is odd but the explicit violation is Ship order occurs after Cancel order The text explicitly says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also it skipped Package items before shipping but the main explicit rule broken is shipping after cancellation Additionally the text says after cancellation it is illogical to continue with fulfillment activities Shipping after cancellation is a clear violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Matches Branch 2 exactly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Violation Ship order occurs directly after Authorize payment skipping Package items The text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order and Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping So skipping packaging violates the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Summary of Violations Case 2 Incorrect ordering of payment authorization and packaging Case 3 Shipping after cancellation and skipping packaging also illogical payment authorization prior to cancellation Case 5 Missing mandatory packaging step before shipping Cases 1 and 4 are fully compliant with the normative process
